title: Kopra Paak (Coconut Burfi)

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Fresh grated coconut – 2 cups (preferably only the white part, finely grated)
Sugar – 1 ½ cups (adjust sweetness if needed)
Saffron strands – 12–15, soaked in 1 tbsp warm milk
Chopped nuts (pistachios, almonds, cashews) – 2 tbsp (for garnish)
Edible silver leaf (varak) – optional, for fine-dining presentation

instruction.title: Prep the Coconut
steps: Ensure coconut is finely grated. Pat dry with a muslin cloth to remove excess moisture if very fresh.

instruction.title: Cook the Base
steps: In a heavy-bottom pan, heat 2 tbsp ghee.
Add grated coconut and sauté on medium flame for 3–4 minutes, until fragrant but not browned. Remove and set aside.

instruction.title: Khoya Mixture
steps: In the same pan, add 1 tbsp ghee and crumble in the khoya.
Cook gently until it turns soft, creamy, and lightly golden (~5 minutes).

instruction.title: Sugar Syrup
steps: In another pan, combine sugar + ½ cup milk.
Heat until sugar dissolves and comes to a 1-string consistency (dip spoon, stretch between fingers – should form one thread).

instruction.title: Combine Everything
steps: Immediately mix syrup with khoya and coconut.
Add saffron milk, cardamom powder, and 1 tbsp ghee.
Stir continuously on medium heat until mixture thickens, leaves the pan, and forms a soft mass.

instruction.title: Set the Burfi
steps: Grease a square tray with ghee.
Pour mixture, flatten evenly with a greased spatula.
Garnish with chopped nuts and edible silver leaf.
Let it cool for 1–2 hours, then cut into neat diamond or square pieces.

chefTips: Use only fresh white coconut for pristine color and delicate texture.
Balance is key: not overly sweet, not overly greasy.
Cook slowly – rushing can caramelize sugar and spoil the flavor.
For an elevated plating, serve one piece with a drizzle of saffron syrup, pistachio dust, and a touch of edible gold.
